task.title: Run Jira Orchestrate for MM-1106
task.instructions:
Run Jira Orchestrate for MM-1106.

Use the trusted Jira preset brief for MM-1106 as the canonical MoonSpec orchestration input.

Classify this input as a single-story feature request. Treat docs/Workflows/CheckpointBranchSystem.md as runtime source requirements, especially sections 5, 17, 18, and 19 and claim IDs docs-workflows-checkpoint-branch-system#s17-rollout, docs-workflows-checkpoint-branch-system#s18-tests, docs-workflows-checkpoint-branch-system#s19-open, and docs-workflows-checkpoint-branch-system#s5-invariants.

Inspect existing MoonSpec artifacts and resume from the first incomplete stage instead of regenerating valid later-stage artifacts. Jira Orchestrate always runs as a runtime implementation workflow; do not implement inline inside this orchestration handoff.

inputs.constraints: Use the trusted Jira preset brief as canonical input. Treat docs/Workflows/CheckpointBranchSystem.md as runtime source requirements. Classify as a single-story feature request. Inspect existing MoonSpec artifacts and resume from the first incomplete stage. Add targeted schema, service, runtime, git, promotion, and provider tests only as implementation slices exist. Keep rollout phases as implementation sequencing rather than durable API behavior. Track unresolved open questions as blockers, assumptions, approval gates, or acceptance criteria rather than hidden fallbacks.

canonicalJiraBriefSummary.summary: Expand tests to cover the remaining checkpoint branch product slices and unresolved decisions
acceptanceCriteria: Schema tests reject missing checkpoint refs, missing instruction refs/digests, post-launch turn mutation, identity conflation, and raw large or sensitive compact state.
Checkpoint validation tests cover valid, missing, corrupted, plan-mismatched, policy-mismatched, and unauthorized checkpoints.
Git isolation tests cover branch creation, deterministic sanitized names, protected refs, idempotent reuse, mismatched metadata failure, and fork isolation.
Runtime tests verify new Step Executions, follow-up turns, forks, branch-turn idempotency keys, and artifact preservation.
Promotion tests cover expected-head validation, gate requirements, invalidation records, and non-deletion of competitors.
Omnigent/provider tests cover fresh-session branch turns, instruction refs, diagnostic-only provider ids, capability-gated same-session continuation rejection, and capture artifact binding.
Capabilities with unresolved design questions are disabled, approval-gated, or represented with explicit clarification evidence.
alreadyImplementedEvidence: tests/unit/services/test_checkpoint_branch_service.py covers source checkpoint validation, child lineage, manifest metadata, branch/turn/artifact/git binding persistence, cross-branch validation, duplicate git work branch rejection, and Step Execution identity distinction.
tests/unit/workflows/temporal/workflows/test_run_step_ledger.py covers generic external_provider_continuation evidence, but not checkpoint-branch-specific provider behavior.
canonicalJiraBriefSummary.remainingWork: The persistence slice has useful unit coverage, but tests for checkpoint validation, runtime branch turns, git isolation, APIs, promotion, UI, and Omnigent branch execution remain missing until those slices exist.
